name: hubspot_search_objects
description: Performs advanced filtered searches across HubSpot object types using complex criteria. Supports complex boolean logic through filter groups. Use this for targeted data retrieval when exact filtering criteria are known. Filter groups are combined with OR logic (ANY can match), while filters within a group are combined with AND logic (ALL must match).

arguments.name: objectType
arguments.description: The type of HubSpot object to search (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, notes, tasks, calls, meetings, emails, products, line_items, quotes). For custom objects, use objectTypeId (e.g., "2-123456") or fullyQualifiedName (e.g., "p123_pets") from hubspot_get_schemas.

arguments.name: query
arguments.description: Text to search across default searchable properties of the specified object type. Each object type has different searchable properties.

arguments.name: limit
arguments.description: The maximum number of results to display per page (max: 100).

arguments.name: after
arguments.description: The paging cursor token of the last successfully read resource.

arguments.name: properties
arguments.description: A list of the properties to be returned in the response.

arguments.name: sorts
arguments.description: A list of sort criteria to apply to the results.

arguments.name: filterGroups
arguments.description: Groups of filters to apply (combined with OR). You can include a maximum of five filterGroups with up to 6 filters in each group.

name: hubspot_batch_create_objects
description: Creates multiple HubSpot objects of the same objectType in a single API call, optimizing for bulk operations. Data Modification Warning: This tool modifies HubSpot data. Only use when the user has explicitly requested to update their CRM.

arguments.name: objectType
arguments.description: The type of HubSpot object to create (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, notes, tasks, calls, meetings, emails, products, line_items, quotes). For custom objects, use objectTypeId (e.g., "2-123456") or fullyQualifiedName (e.g., "p123_pets") from hubspot_get_schemas.

arguments.name: inputs
arguments.description: Array of objects to create (maximum 100 per batch)

name: hubspot_batch_read_objects
description: Retrieves multiple HubSpot objects of the same object type by their IDs in a single batch operation. Use this tool to retrieve objects when the object IDs are known.

arguments.name: objectType
arguments.description: The type of HubSpot object to read (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, notes, tasks, calls, meetings, emails, products, line_items, quotes). For custom objects, use objectTypeId (e.g., "2-123456") or fullyQualifiedName (e.g., "p123_pets") from hubspot_get_schemas.

arguments.name: inputs
arguments.description: Array of object IDs to read (maximum 100 per batch)

arguments.name: properties
arguments.description: Optional list of property names to include in the results

arguments.name: propertiesWithHistory
arguments.description: Optional list of property names to include with history

name: hubspot_batch_update_objects
description: Updates multiple existing HubSpot objects of the same objectType in a single API call. Data Modification Warning: This tool modifies HubSpot data. Only use when the user has explicitly requested to update their CRM.

arguments.name: objectType
arguments.description: The type of HubSpot object to update (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, notes, tasks, calls, meetings, emails, products, line_items, quotes). For custom objects, use objectTypeId (e.g., "2-123456") or fullyQualifiedName (e.g., "p123_pets") from hubspot_get_schemas.

arguments.name: inputs
arguments.description: Array of objects to update (maximum 100 per batch)

name: hubspot_list_objects
description: Retrieves a paginated list of objects of a specified type from HubSpot. Use for initial data exploration to understand the data structure of a HubSpot object type. Helps list objects when the search criteria or filter criteria is not clear.

arguments.name: objectType
arguments.description: The type of HubSpot object to list (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, notes, tasks, calls, meetings, emails, products, line_items, quotes). For custom objects, use objectTypeId (e.g., "2-123456") or fullyQualifiedName (e.g., "p123_pets") from hubspot_get_schemas.

arguments.name: limit
arguments.description: The maximum number of results to display per page (max: 500).

arguments.name: after
arguments.description: The paging cursor token of the last successfully read resource.

arguments.name: properties
arguments.description: A list of the properties to be returned in the response.

arguments.name: associations
arguments.description: A list of object types to retrieve associated IDs for.

arguments.name: archived
arguments.description: Whether to return only results that have been archived.

name: hubspot_get_schemas
description: Retrieves all custom object schemas defined in the HubSpot account. Use before working with custom objects to understand available object types, their properties, and associations.

name: hubspot_batch_create_associations
description: Establishes relationships between HubSpot objects, linking records across different object types, by creating associations between objects in batch. Data Modification Warning: This tool modifies HubSpot data. Only use when the user has explicitly requested to update their CRM.

arguments.name: fromObjectType
arguments.description: The type of HubSpot object to create association from (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, notes, tasks, calls, meetings, emails, products, line_items, quotes). For custom objects, use objectTypeId (e.g., "2-123456") or fullyQualifiedName (e.g., "p123_pets") from hubspot_get_schemas.

arguments.name: toObjectType
arguments.description: The type of HubSpot object to create association to (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, notes, tasks, calls, meetings, emails, products, line_items, quotes). For custom objects, use objectTypeId (e.g., "2-123456") or fullyQualifiedName (e.g., "p123_pets") from hubspot_get_schemas.

arguments.name: types
arguments.description: The types of associations to create

arguments.name: inputs
arguments.description: List of association inputs defining the relationships to create (max 100 associations per batch)

name: hubspot_get_association_definitions
description: Retrieves valid association types between specific HubSpot object types. Always use before creating associations to ensure valid relationship types or to help troubleshoot association creation errors.

arguments.name: fromObjectType
arguments.description: The type of HubSpot object to get association from (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, notes, tasks, calls, meetings, emails, products, line_items, quotes). For custom objects, use objectTypeId (e.g., "2-123456") or fullyQualifiedName (e.g., "p123_pets") from hubspot_get_schemas.

arguments.name: toObjectType
arguments.description: The type of HubSpot object to get association to (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, notes, tasks, calls, meetings, emails, products, line_items, quotes). For custom objects, use objectTypeId (e.g., "2-123456") or fullyQualifiedName (e.g., "p123_pets") from hubspot_get_schemas.

name: hubspot_list_associations
description: Retrieves existing relationships between a specific object and other objects of a particular type. For example, you can find all companies that a contact is associated with, all deals related to a company, or discover which customers have an open ticket.

arguments.name: objectType
arguments.description: The type of HubSpot object to get associations from (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, notes, tasks, calls, meetings, emails, products, line_items, quotes). For custom objects, use objectTypeId (e.g., "2-123456") or fullyQualifiedName (e.g., "p123_pets") from hubspot_get_schemas.

arguments.name: objectId
arguments.description: The ID of the HubSpot object to get associations from

arguments.name: toObjectType
arguments.description: The type of HubSpot object to get associations to (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, notes, tasks, calls, meetings, emails, products, line_items, quotes). For custom objects, use objectTypeId (e.g., "2-123456") or fullyQualifiedName (e.g., "p123_pets") from hubspot_get_schemas.

arguments.name: after
arguments.description: Paging cursor token for retrieving the next page of results

name: hubspot_get_lists
description: Searches HubSpot lists in the account. Can filter by name (query) and processing type (MANUAL, DYNAMIC, SNAPSHOT). Returns list ID, name, object type, processing type, and size.

arguments.name: query
arguments.description: Optional text to search for in list names.

arguments.name: processingTypes
arguments.description: Optional filter by processing type. MANUAL = static lists, DYNAMIC = auto-updating lists, SNAPSHOT = one-time filter lists. If not specified, returns all types.

arguments.name: offset
arguments.description: The offset for pagination. Use to retrieve the next page of results.

name: hubspot_get_list_memberships
description: Retrieves all record IDs that belong to a specific HubSpot list. Returns record IDs and when they were added to the list.

arguments.name: listId
arguments.description: The ID of the HubSpot list to retrieve memberships for.

arguments.name: limit
arguments.description: The maximum number of record IDs to return per page (max: 500).

arguments.name: after
arguments.description: The paging cursor token of the last successfully read resource.

name: hubspot_update_list_memberships
description: Adds or removes records from a static (MANUAL) HubSpot list. Data Modification Warning: This tool modifies HubSpot list memberships. Only use when the user has explicitly requested to update list memberships. Note: Only works with static lists, not dynamic or snapshot lists.

arguments.name: listId
arguments.description: The ID of the static HubSpot list to modify.

arguments.name: action
arguments.description: Whether to add or remove records from the list.

arguments.name: recordIds
arguments.description: Array of record IDs to add or remove (maximum 500 per request).

name: hubspot_create_list
description: Creates a new HubSpot list (segment). Use processingType 'MANUAL' for static lists where you'll add records manually, or 'DYNAMIC' for lists that auto-update based on filter criteria. After creating a static list, use hubspot_update_list_memberships to add records. Data Modification Warning: This tool creates HubSpot lists. Only use when the user has explicitly requested to create a list.

arguments.name: name
arguments.description: The name of the list. Must be unique across all public lists in the account.

arguments.name: objectTypeId
arguments.description: The object type ID for records in this list. Common values: '0-1' (contacts), '0-2' (companies), '0-3' (deals), '0-5' (tickets).

arguments.name: processingType
arguments.description: MANUAL = static list (add records via API), DYNAMIC = auto-updates based on filterBranch criteria, SNAPSHOT = filters once then becomes static.

arguments.name: filterBranch
arguments.description: Filter definition for DYNAMIC or SNAPSHOT lists. Structure: top-level filterBranchType 'OR' with filterBranches array containing 'AND' branches. Each filter requires: filterType, property, and operation object (with operationType and operator). Search HubSpot list filter API docs for valid operators.

name: hubspot_batch_delete_objects
description: Archives (soft-deletes) multiple HubSpot objects of the same type in a single batch operation. Archived objects can be restored from the HubSpot UI within 90 days. Data Modification Warning: This tool permanently archives objects and should be used with extreme caution. Only use when the user has explicitly requested to delete/archive objects.

arguments.name: objectType
arguments.description: The type of HubSpot object to archive (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, notes, tasks, calls, meetings, emails, products, line_items, quotes). For custom objects, use objectTypeId (e.g., "2-123456") or fullyQualifiedName (e.g., "p123_pets") from hubspot_get_schemas.

arguments.name: inputs
arguments.description: Array of object IDs to archive (maximum 100 per batch)

name: hubspot_batch_delete_associations
description: Removes associations (relationships) between HubSpot objects in batch. Data Modification Warning: This tool permanently removes relationships between objects. Only use when the user has explicitly requested to remove associations.

arguments.name: fromObjectType
arguments.description: The type of HubSpot object to remove associations from (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, notes, tasks, calls, meetings, emails, products, line_items, quotes). For custom objects, use objectTypeId (e.g., "2-123456") or fullyQualifiedName (e.g., "p123_pets") from hubspot_get_schemas.

arguments.name: toObjectType
arguments.description: The type of HubSpot object to remove associations to (e.g., contacts, companies, deals, tickets, notes, tasks, calls, meetings, emails, products, line_items, quotes). For custom objects, use objectTypeId (e.g., "2-123456") or fullyQualifiedName (e.g., "p123_pets") from hubspot_get_schemas.

arguments.name: inputs
arguments.description: Array of association pairs to remove (maximum 100 per batch)
